channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Świątek sheepish after training accident injures coach
item.description: Francisco Roig will think twice about betting against his new charge Iga Świątek after the veteran coach tore his Achilles tendon in a volley session in Rome.

media:group.media:description: Iga Świątek did not immediately realise how bad her coach's injury was.

item.title: Spying controversy hits richest prize in world football
item.description: Southampton has been accused of spying on rivals Middlesbrough ahead of their crucial Championship play-off clash at the Riverside on Saturday.

media:group.media:description: Southampton will get to grips with Middlesbrough in the play-off semifinal this weekend.

item.title: Much-loved tramway Clydesdales hit with mystery flu-like illness
item.description: The majestic horses that pull the historic tramway at Victor Harbor on the South Australian coastline are under the weather. Locals hope they will soon return.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Horn

media:group.media:description: Archie is resting while he shakes off his illness.

item.title: Client abuse and excessive workloads impacting vets
item.description: A parliamentary inquiry makes more than 30 recommendations to address increase of poor veterinary mental health outcomes.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kelly

media:group.media:description: Sophie Putland had a lifelong love for all animals, but especially horses.

item.title: 'My heart nearly stopped': Rare new bug spotted three times since discovery
item.description: A new type of bug, celebrated for the "really cool" way it assassinates prey, has been named in honour of traditional custodians in the northern Australia region where it was discovered.

media:group.media:description: The Larrakia feather-legged assassin was recently discovered among leaf litter in the Northern Territory.

item.title: Live: US fires on tankers as Tehran accuses Washington of 'reckless military adventure'
item.description: The US military has struck two empty Iranian-flagged oil tankers, US Central Command says, adding that a third Iranian-flagged vessel had been disabled on Wednesday. Follow live.

media:group.media:description: US forces have disabled two vessels.

item.title: Gold Coast couple ditches new build for 100-year-old home
item.description: Kim and Michael Kendall's Palm Beach home was sinking. Instead of a new build, the couple decided to relocate and renovate a century-old Queenslander.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Mahe

media:group.media:description: The Queenslander is turning heads in Palm Beach.

item.title: Trump releases UFO files in 'unprecedented transparency'
item.description: The US government releases 160 previously classified files on alleged UFO sightings and alien life, including a photo of "unidentified phenomena" taken from the Moon's surface in 1969.

media:group.media:description: A sketch from eyewitness reports in September 2023, released by the Defense Department.

item.title: Tasmania rejected an on‑country hearing but other courts show how it works
item.description: A Tasmanian Aboriginal woman's request to have her court hearing held on country was rejected. But on-country hearings have been held in other jurisdictions. So how do they work?
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Rojahn

media:group.media:description: Ruth Langford wanted the hearing for her court matter regarding participation in anti-logging protests to be held on Aboriginal-owned land.

item.title: Investigators head to Geraldton as dust fears rise with iron ore expansion
item.description: Geraldton residents say dust from expanding iron ore operations is blanketing homes, boats and businesses, fuelling fears the coastal city could turn into "the next Port Hedland".
item.dc:creator: Joanna Prendergast, Chris Lewis, and Piper Duffy

media:group.media:description: Dean Parker has erected a protest sign outside his shop at Geraldton's Fishermen's Wharf.

item.title: Former ACT politician allegedly groomed teen with dinners, alcohol
item.description: Court documents reveal allegations former ACT attorney-general Gordon Ramsay, who is charged with grooming a boy, allegedly set up an account on the cloud for the teen to share photos in increasing states of undress.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Byrne

media:group.media:description: Court documents allege Gordon Ramsay befriended the boy before requesting sexualised images.

item.title: Julie Bishop was adamant she wasn't going anywhere — until she did
item.description: December 31 was the date when Julie Bishop was due to finish her term as Australian National University chancellor, so why — after having repeatedly stated she would see her commitment through — did she leave months early?
item.dc:creator: Adam Shirley

media:group.media:description: Julie Bishop was the university's 13th chancellor and had held the role since 2020.

item.title: The Devil Wears Prada 2 is a tepid re-heat of the original
item.description: Miranda Priestly has lost her fangs in the reboot as weak plot lines make room for superfluous scenes and characters.
item.dc:creator: Virginia Trioli

media:group.media:description: The film is a tepid re-heat of old character clashes and foibles that decades of work experience seem to have left unaffected.

item.title: F1 agrees engine changes for 'safer, fairer' racing
item.description: Formula 1 bosses have agreed to an engine shift that will see cars have more combustion power and less electric in 2027 in response to feedback from drivers.

media:group.media:description: Engines will return to being combustion dominant as soon as next year.

item.title: The long road to recovery after concussion
item.description: Liam Dennis was at the top of his game when, in 2023, a crushing blow to the head brought his football career to a halt.
item.dc:creator: Duncan Bailey

media:group.media:description: Liam Dennis said it was hard to have to quit football so suddenly.

item.title: Extraordinary question a sign of Minns's deteriorating relationship with chamber
item.description: The NSW premier faced an extraordinary question this week amid a deteriorating relationship between his government and the Legislative Council.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Lewis

media:group.media:description: NSW Premier Chris Minns faced an extraordinary question this week about the Legislative Council.

item.title: Australia is breaking its carbon bond, but there's a cost
item.description: As Australia weans itself away from coal, aging power stations across the country have been reduced to smoke and rubble. But for communities at the coalface, there are serious and dramatic consequences.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Keane, Leah MacLennan, and Will Hunter

media:group.media:description: The moment the 200m chimney stack at the Northern Power Station site in Port Augusta was felled in 2018.

item.title: De Minaur's disastrous clay court form continues
item.description: Struggling Alex de Minaur suffers another early clay-court tournament exit, this time at the Italian Open in Rome.

media:group.media:description: Alex de Minaur's poor clay court form continues.

item.title: Fears Deaf community will 'miss out' on news with Auslan program to end
item.description: News delivered by Deaf journalists covering international wars, elections, cyclones, and fuel shortages is set to wind up next month due to a federal government grant ending.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Richards

media:group.media:description: Debbie Kennewell is one of the Deaf presenters for Auslan90's news bulletins.

item.title: Victoria's treaty leaders are here and won't shy away from a challenge
item.description: The new leaders of Victoria's First Peoples' Assembly say they're pushing ahead with promises made under the state's treaty, despite political uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dunstan, Kate Ashton, and Nate Woodall

media:group.media:description: Meriki Onus and Djaran Murray-Jackson have been elected co-chairs of Victoria's First Peoples' Assembly.

item.title: Farrer by-election candidates walk immigration policy tightrope
item.description: Many Farrer industries rely heavily on migration, while some communities face housing supply issues, making immigration a challenging topic for candidates.
item.dc:creator: Erin Somerville and Philippe Perez

media:group.media:description: Patrick Bourke says common sense needs to applied to Farrer's immigration policy.

item.title: Depth of antisemitism exposed by anonymous witnesses before commission
item.description: Fear, concerns for safety and the impact on children dominated the first week of hearings of the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion.
item.dc:creator: Phoebe Pin

media:group.media:description: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion's first week of public hearings wrapped up on Friday.

item.title: Huge crash sees Aussie sprint hope hit deck in Giro d'Italia opener
item.description: Kaden Groves hit the tarmac hard in a catastrophic high-speed crash in the final metres of the first stage of the Giro d'Italia.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Kaden Groves was battered and bruised but should be OK to continue the race.

item.title: Ukraine and Russia agree to three-day ceasefire, Trump says
item.description: US President Donald Trump takes to social media to announce a three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine from May 9 to 11.

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump has announced a ceasefire.

item.title: Disability support group harvests last Mother's Day bloom after 35 years
item.description: Disability support organisation Endeavour Foundation in Bundaberg is changing its business model away from growing flowers to packaging solutions.
item.dc:creator: Grace Whiteside

media:group.media:description: Shaun Nortje says extreme weather and a changing business model have contributed to the decision.
